steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Text 1 claims social structures progressed linearly: from equal-status hunter-gatherer bands → through agriculture-driven population growth → to hierarchical stages (clans, chiefdoms, states). Graeber and Wengrow's response hinges on whether they accept or challenge this linear progression.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Graeber and Wengrow directly challenge the linear progression framework—their evidence of hierarchy 50,000 years ago shows social systems have always been flexible, not progressing through stages. The other choices either frame the disagreement differently, accept conventional timelines, or address something not in question. That matches choice B — B is correct.
